# qrdev

> Zero-dependency CLI to access your local dev server from any device on your network via QR code.

## What it does

qrdev wraps any dev server command, detects the port it runs on, finds your local network IP, and displays a QR code in the terminal. Scan the QR code from any phone, tablet, or device on the same Wi-Fi network to instantly open your dev server — no manual IP typing needed.

## Use Cases

- Access localhost from your phone during development
- Test responsive design on a real mobile device
- Share your local dev server with others on the same network
- Preview your website on mobile without deploying
- QR code for localhost

## Key Features

- Zero dependencies — nothing extra to install
- Works with any dev server: Vite, Next.js, Nuxt, Svelte, Express, Python, Ruby, etc.
- Works with any package manager: npm, bun, yarn, pnpm
- No config files, no plugins, no framework lock-in
- Just prepend `qrdev` to your existing dev command

## Install

```bash
npm i -g qrdev
```

## Usage

```bash
qrdev npm run dev
qrdev vite
qrdev next dev
qrdev bun run dev
qrdev yarn dev
qrdev pnpm dev
qrdev node server.js
qrdev python -m http.server 8000
```

## Per-project Setup

```bash
npm i -D qrdev
```

Then in package.json:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "qrdev vite"
  }
}
```

## Requirements

- Node.js >= 14
- Both devices on the same Wi-Fi / local network

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/qrdev
- GitHub: https://github.com/aysahoo/qrdev
